meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Debugging Code Fast for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master API integration to resolve debugging bottlenecks. A high-efficiency, intermediate tutorial for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop wrestling with opaque error logs. By wrapping your API integration in a robust observability layer using Antigravity for automated request tracing and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you turn black-box failures into actionable data. This systematic approach isolates the handshake failure, fixes the schema mismatch, and restores your deployment velocity.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your client delivery, and you’re staring at a cryptic 500 error that refuses to yield. It’s the classic developer nightmare: the API documentation says one thing, your integration logic says another, and your debugging process has become a slow, manual game of trial and error that is eating your profit margins.

But here is the secret: professional-grade debugging isn't about staring harder at the code. It’s about building a feedback loop that does the heavy lifting for you. In this guide, we’ll replace your manual debugging workflows with an automated, AI-assisted architecture that turns integration failures into solved tickets in minutes, not hours.

steps.title: Define the Integration Schema
steps.description: Before writing logic, generate a strict TypeScript interface based on the API response to prevent type-mismatch errors before they occur.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided API documentation and generate a strict TypeScript interface definition that handles nested objects and potential null values safely.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a TS interface for the API response provided in the context.'

steps.title: Implement Observability Wrapper
steps.description: Use Antigravity to inject a logging layer around your fetch calls to capture headers, payloads, and raw response bodies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a wrapper function for my API client that logs outgoing requests and incoming responses, including timing and status codes, for debugging purposes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file api-client.ts --instruction 'Wrap all outgoing requests in a logging function.'

steps.title: Automate Error Simulation
steps.description: Create a suite of mock scenarios to ensure your error handling logic triggers correctly when the API returns unexpected data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that mocks API failure scenarios, specifically 401, 403, and 429 status codes, to verify my error handling logic.

steps.title: Refine Logic with Claude Code
steps.description: Feed the captured logs from your wrapper into Claude Code to pinpoint the exact line of logic causing the integration failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the captured log file and identify the specific discrepancy between the expected schema and the actual API response.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Run the automated verification script to confirm the fix works in the dev environment before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment verification script that runs a health check on the newly integrated API endpoint.

pro_tips: Always log the full request URL and headers, not just the body; 90% of integration errors are authentication header issues.
Use a circuit breaker pattern in your API client to prevent your app from crashing during downstream service outages.
Never rely on production logs for initial debugging; use a local proxy like Antigravity to capture traffic in your dev environment.
Keep your API client logic in a separate package or folder so you can version control it independently of your UI.
Configure your AI agent to output logs in JSON format for easier parsing by log aggregation tools like Datadog or ELK.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is stable, move on to implementing rate-limiting and caching layers to optimize your application's performance and reduce external API costs.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to stop guessing and start fixing. Stop wasting billable hours on avoidable bugs and start delivering robust, production-ready code that keeps your clients happy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
